new_terms.context: DRAM has benefited from the two scaling laws that defined the semiconductor industry: Moore’s Law and Dennard scaling.
The 1T1C DRAM cell, with one access transistor and one storage capacitor, scaled for decades.
The 1T1C DRAM cell, with one access transistor and one storage capacitor, scaled for decades.
Capacitors are now extreme three-dimensional structures with aspect ratios approaching 100:1
Bitlines and sense amplifiers, once secondary concerns, are now dominant constraints.
Every incremental shrink reduces signal margin, increases variability, and raises cost.
The Memory Wall: Past, Present, and Future of DRAM
